ansi(3) Library Functions Manual ansi(3)

NAME

ansi - ANSI Utilities

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct ansi_rle_context_t
struct ansi_timing_t
struct rgb_error_t

Enumerations


enum ansi_color_mode_t { ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND = 0, ANSI_MODE_BACKGROUND, ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND_BACKGROUND }

Functions


char * ansi_strip_escapes (const char *input, size_t input_len)
Strip all ANSI escape sequences from a string. bool ansi_is_already_colorized (const char *message, size_t pos)
Check if a position in text is already colored. char * append_truecolor_fg (char *dst, uint8_t r, uint8_t g, uint8_t b)
char * append_truecolor_bg (char *dst, uint8_t r, uint8_t g, uint8_t b)
char * append_truecolor_fg_bg (char *dst, uint8_t fg_r, uint8_t fg_g, uint8_t fg_b, uint8_t bg_r, uint8_t bg_g, uint8_t bg_b)
void ansi_rle_init (ansi_rle_context_t *ctx, char *buffer, size_t capacity, ansi_color_mode_t mode)
void ansi_rle_add_pixel (ansi_rle_context_t *ctx, uint8_t r, uint8_t g, uint8_t b, char ascii_char)
void ansi_rle_finish (ansi_rle_context_t *ctx)
void ansi_fast_init (void)
void ansi_fast_init_256color (void)
void ansi_fast_init_16color (void)
char * append_256color_fg (char *dst, uint8_t color_index)
char * append_256color_bg (char *dst, uint8_t color_index)
uint8_t rgb_to_256color (uint8_t r, uint8_t g, uint8_t b)
char * append_16color_fg (char *dst, uint8_t color_index)
char * append_16color_bg (char *dst, uint8_t color_index)
uint8_t rgb_to_16color (uint8_t r, uint8_t g, uint8_t b)
void get_16color_rgb (uint8_t color_index, uint8_t *r, uint8_t *g, uint8_t *b)
uint8_t rgb_to_16color_dithered (int r, int g, int b, int x, int y, int width, int height, rgb_error_t *error_buffer)
char * append_color_fg_for_mode (char *dst, uint8_t r, uint8_t g, uint8_t b, terminal_color_mode_t mode)

Detailed Description

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum ansi_color_mode_t

#include <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>

Enumerator

ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND
ANSI_MODE_BACKGROUND
ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND_BACKGROUND

36              {
37   ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND = 0,
38   ANSI_MODE_BACKGROUND,
39   ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND_BACKGROUND,
40 } ansi_color_mode_t;










Function
  Documentation




void
  ansi_fast_init (void )


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Initialize fast
    ANSI generation (call once at startup)


Definition at line 295 of file ansi.c.


295                           {
296   // Initialize the dec3 cache used by truecolor functions
297   ascii_simd_init();
298 }

void
  ansi_fast_init_16color (void )


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Initialize
    16-color mode (thread-safe, called on first use)


Definition at line 374 of file ansi.c.


374                                   {
375   if (!lifecycle_init(&g_color16_lc, "ansi_16color")) {
376     return; // Already initialized
377   }
378   do_init_16color();
379 }

    session_display_create().

bool ansi_is_already_colorized (const char * message,
  size_t pos)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>


Check if a position in text is already colored. Scans from the
    start of the message to the given position, tracking ANSI escape codes.
    Returns true if there is an active color code (not in reset state).




Detects reset codes: \x1b[0m and \x1b[m




Parameters


message The full message


pos Position to check


Returns


true if already colored (not in reset state), false if in
  reset state


Definition at line 74 of file ansi.c.


74                                                                 {
75   if (!message) {
76     return false;
77   }
78 
79   bool in_reset = true; // Start in reset state
80 
81   // Scan from beginning to current position looking for ANSI codes
82   for (size_t i = 0; i < pos && message[i] != '\0'; i++) {
83     if (message[i] == '\x1b' && message[i + 1] == '[') {
84       // Found ANSI escape sequence start
85       // Look for the end marker 'm'
86       size_t j = i + 2;
87       while (message[j] != '\0' && message[j] != 'm') {
88         j++;
89       }
90 
91       if (message[j] == 'm') {
92         // Extract the color code part (between [ and m)
93         // Check if it's a reset code: \x1b[0m or \x1b[m
94         in_reset = ((j == i + 3 && message[i + 2] == '0') || (j == i + 2));
95       }
96     }
97   }
98 
99   // Return true if NOT in reset state (already colorized)
100   return !in_reset;
101 }






Referenced by colorize_log_message().






void
  ansi_rle_add_pixel (ansi_rle_context_t * ctx, uint8_t r,
  uint8_t g, uint8_t b, char ascii_char)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Add pixel with
    run-length encoding (only emits SGR when color changes)


Parameters


ctx RLE context


r Red component


g Green component


b Blue component


ascii_char ASCII character to append


Definition at line 237 of file ansi.c.


237                                                                                                    {
238   // Check if we need to emit a new SGR sequence
239   bool color_changed = ctx->first_pixel || (r != ctx->last_r) || (g != ctx->last_g) || (b != ctx->last_b);
240 
241   if (color_changed) {
242     if (ctx->length + 40 >= ctx->capacity) {
243       // Log when we can't add a color change due to buffer capacity
244       log_warn_every(US_PER_SEC_INT, "RLE_CAPACITY: Cannot add SGR at length=%zu, capacity=%zu (need 40 bytes)",
245                      ctx->length, ctx->capacity);
246     }
247   }
248 
249   if (color_changed && (ctx->length + 40 < ctx->capacity)) { // Reserve 40 bytes for SGR (FG+BG max is 38)
250     char *pos = ctx->buffer + ctx->length;
251 
252     switch (ctx->mode) {
253     case ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND:
254       pos = append_truecolor_fg(pos, r, g, b);
255       break;
256     case ANSI_MODE_BACKGROUND:
257       pos = append_truecolor_bg(pos, r, g, b);
258       break;
259     case ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND_BACKGROUND:
260       // For FG+BG mode, use a default background (black) or implement dual-color logic
261       pos = append_truecolor_fg_bg(pos, r, g, b, 0, 0, 0);
262       break;
263     }
264 
265     ctx->length = (size_t)(pos - ctx->buffer);
266     ctx->last_r = r;
267     ctx->last_g = g;
268     ctx->last_b = b;
269     ctx->first_pixel = false;
270   }
271 
272   // Add the ASCII character
273   if (ctx->length < ctx->capacity - 1) {
274     ctx->buffer[ctx->length++] = ascii_char;
275   }
276 }






References ANSI_MODE_BACKGROUND,
    ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND, ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND_BACKGROUND,
    append_truecolor_bg(), append_truecolor_fg(),
    append_truecolor_fg_bg(), ansi_rle_context_t::buffer,
    ansi_rle_context_t::capacity, ansi_rle_context_t::first_pixel,
    ansi_rle_context_t::last_b, ansi_rle_context_t::last_g,
    ansi_rle_context_t::last_r, ansi_rle_context_t::length,
    log_warn_every, ansi_rle_context_t::mode, and
    US_PER_SEC_INT.


Referenced by image_print_color().






void
  ansi_rle_finish (ansi_rle_context_t * ctx)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Finish RLE
    sequence with reset and null terminator


Parameters


ctx RLE context


Definition at line 279 of file ansi.c.


279                                               {
280   // Add reset sequence (requires 4 bytes + 1 for null terminator = 5 bytes total)
281   if (ctx->length + 5 <= ctx->capacity) {
282     SAFE_MEMCPY(ctx->buffer + ctx->length, 4, "\033[0m", 4);
283     ctx->length += 4;
284   }
285 
286   // Null terminate
287   if (ctx->length < ctx->capacity) {
288     ctx->buffer[ctx->length] = '\0';
289   }
290 }






References ansi_rle_context_t::buffer,
    ansi_rle_context_t::capacity, ansi_rle_context_t::length, and
    SAFE_MEMCPY.


Referenced by image_print_color().






void
  ansi_rle_init (ansi_rle_context_t * ctx, char * buffer, size_t
  capacity, ansi_color_mode_t mode)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Initialize RLE
    context for efficient ANSI generation


Parameters


ctx Context to initialize


buffer Output buffer


capacity Buffer capacity


mode Color mode (foreground/background/both)


Definition at line 224 of file ansi.c.


224                                                                                                    {
225   ctx->buffer = buffer;
226   ctx->capacity = capacity;
227   ctx->length = 0;
228   ctx->mode = mode;
229   ctx->first_pixel = true;
230   // Initialize with impossible color values to force first SGR
231   ctx->last_r = 0xFF;
232   ctx->last_g = 0xFF;
233   ctx->last_b = 0xFF;
234 }






References ansi_rle_context_t::buffer,
    ansi_rle_context_t::capacity, ansi_rle_context_t::first_pixel,
    ansi_rle_context_t::last_b, ansi_rle_context_t::last_g,
    ansi_rle_context_t::last_r, ansi_rle_context_t::length, and
    ansi_rle_context_t::mode.


Referenced by image_print_color().






char
  * ansi_strip_escapes (const char * input, size_t input_len)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>


Strip all ANSI escape sequences from a string. Removes all ANSI
    CSI sequences (ESC [ ... final_byte) from the input, leaving only printable
    text. Useful for creating plain text output from colorized ASCII art.




Parameters


input Input string containing ANSI escape
  sequences


input_len Length of input string


Returns


Newly allocated string with escapes removed (caller must
  free), or NULL on error


Definition at line 23 of file ansi.c.


23                                                               {
24   if (!input || input_len == 0) {
25     return NULL;
26   }
27 
28   // Allocate buffer directly for output (at most input_len bytes)
29   char *output = SAFE_MALLOC(input_len + 1, char *);
30   if (!output) {
31     return NULL;
32   }
33 
34   size_t out_pos = 0;
35   size_t i = 0;
36 
37   while (i < input_len) {
38     // Check for ESC character (start of ANSI sequence)
39     if (input[i] == '\x1b' && i + 1 < input_len && input[i + 1] == '[') {
40       // Skip ESC[
41       i += 2;
42 
43       // Skip parameter bytes (digits, semicolons, and intermediate bytes)
44       while (i < input_len) {
45         char c = input[i];
46         // Parameter bytes: 0x30-0x3F (digits, semicolon, etc.)
47         // Intermediate bytes: 0x20-0x2F (space, !, ", etc.)
48         if ((c >= 0x30 && c <= 0x3F) || (c >= 0x20 && c <= 0x2F)) {
49           i++;
50         } else {
51           break;
52         }
53       }
54 
55       // Skip final byte (0x40-0x7E: @, A-Z, [, \, ], ^, _, `, a-z, {, |, }, ~)
56       if (i < input_len) {
57         char final = input[i];
58         if (final >= 0x40 && final <= 0x7E) {
59           i++;
60         }
61       }
62     } else {
63       // Regular character - copy to output
64       output[out_pos++] = input[i];
65       i++;
66     }
67   }
68 
69   // Null-terminate the output
70   output[out_pos] = '\0';
71   return output;
72 }






References SAFE_MALLOC.


Referenced by display_height(), display_width(),
    layout_print_two_column_row(), and log_mmap_write().






char
  * append_16color_bg (char * dst, uint8_t color_index)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Append 16-color
    ANSI background SGR sequence


Parameters


dst Destination buffer


color_index 16-color index (0-15)


Returns


Pointer to end of written data


Definition at line 397 of file ansi.c.


397                                                         {
398   // Ensure initialization before use
399   ansi_fast_init_16color();
400 
401   if (color_index >= 16) {
402     color_index = 0; // Default to black background
403   }
404 
405   const char *color_str = color16_bg_strings[color_index];
406   while (*color_str) {
407     *dst++ = *color_str++;
408   }
409 
410   return dst;
411 }






References ansi_fast_init_16color().


Referenced by
    image_print_16color_dithered_with_background(), and
    rgb_to_16color_halfblocks_scalar().

384

387   }
388 
389   const char *color_str = color16_fg_strings[color_index];

char
  * append_color_fg_for_mode (char * dst, uint8_t r, uint8_t g,
  uint8_t b, terminal_color_mode_t mode)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Append color SGR
    sequence for the appropriate terminal color mode


Parameters


dst Destination buffer


r Red component


g Green component


b Blue component


mode Terminal color mode capability


Returns


Pointer to end of written data


Definition at line 564 of file ansi.c.


564                                                                                                        {
565   switch (mode) {
566   case COLOR_MODE_TRUECOLOR:
567     return append_truecolor_fg(dst, r, g, b);
568 
569   case COLOR_MODE_256_COLOR: {
570     uint8_t color_index = rgb_to_256color(r, g, b);
571     return append_256color_fg(dst, color_index);
572   }
573 
574   case COLOR_MODE_16_COLOR: {
575     uint8_t color_index = rgb_to_16color(r, g, b);
576     return append_16color_fg(dst, color_index);
577   }
578 
579   case COLOR_MODE_NONE:
580   case COLOR_MODE_AUTO:
581   default:
582     // No color output for monochrome mode or auto mode (fallback)
583     return dst;
584   }
585 }






References append_16color_fg(),
    append_256color_fg(), append_truecolor_fg(),
    COLOR_MODE_16_COLOR, COLOR_MODE_256_COLOR,
    COLOR_MODE_AUTO, COLOR_MODE_NONE, COLOR_MODE_TRUECOLOR,
    rgb_to_16color(), and rgb_to_256color().






char
  * append_truecolor_bg (char * dst, uint8_t r, uint8_t g,
  uint8_t b)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Append truecolor
    background SGR sequence Maximum output: 19 bytes
    (\033[48;2;255;255;255m)


Parameters


dst Destination buffer


r Red component


g Green component


b Blue component


Returns


Pointer to end of written data


Definition at line 149 of file ansi.c.


149                                                                       {
150   // Ensure dec3 cache is initialized
151   if (!g_dec3_cache.dec3_initialized) {
152     init_dec3();
153   }
154 
155   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 19, "\033[48;2;", 7);
156   dst += 7;
157 
158   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 12, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[r].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[r].len);
159   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[r].len;
160   *dst++ = ';';
161 
162   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 8, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[g].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[g].len);
163   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[g].len;
164   *dst++ = ';';
165 
166   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 4, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[b].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[b].len);
167   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[b].len;
168   *dst++ = 'm';
169 
170   return dst;
171 }

g Green component

Pointer to end of written data


Definition at line 119 of file ansi.c.


119                                                                       {
120   // Ensure dec3 cache is initialized
121   if (!g_dec3_cache.dec3_initialized) {
122     init_dec3();
123   }
124 
125   // Static prefix - 7 bytes
126   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 19, "\033[38;2;", 7);
127   dst += 7;
128 
129   // Red component + semicolon
130   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 12, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[r].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[r].len);
131   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[r].len;
132   *dst++ = ';';
133 
134   // Green component + semicolon
135   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 8, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[g].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[g].len);
136   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[g].len;
137   *dst++ = ';';
138 
139   // Blue component + suffix
140   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 4, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[b].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[b].len);
141   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[b].len;
142   *dst++ = 'm';
143 
144   return dst;
145 }

  uint8_t bg_b)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Append combined
    foreground + background truecolor SGR Maximum output: 38 bytes
    (\033[38;2;255;255;255;48;2;255;255;255m)


Parameters


dst Destination buffer


fg_r Foreground red component


fg_g Foreground green component


fg_b Foreground blue component


bg_r Background red component


bg_g Background green component


bg_b Background blue component


Returns


Pointer to end of written data

176                                            {
177   // Ensure dec3 cache is initialized
178   if (!g_dec3_cache.dec3_initialized) {
179     init_dec3();
180   }
181 
182   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 38, "\033[38;2;", 7);
183   dst += 7;
184 
185   // Foreground RGB (remaining: 31 bytes max)
186   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 31, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[fg_r].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[fg_r].len);
187   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[fg_r].len;
188   *dst++ = ';';
189 
190   // Remaining: 27 bytes max
191   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 27, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[fg_g].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[fg_g].len);
192   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[fg_g].len;
193   *dst++ = ';';
194 
195   // Remaining: 23 bytes max
196   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 23, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[fg_b].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[fg_b].len);
197   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[fg_b].len;
198 
199   // Background RGB (remaining: 20 bytes max)
200   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 20, ";48;2;", 6);
201   dst += 6;
202 
203   // Remaining: 14 bytes max
204   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 14, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[bg_r].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[bg_r].len);
205   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[bg_r].len;
206   *dst++ = ';';
207 
208   // Remaining: 10 bytes max
209   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 10, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[bg_g].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[bg_g].len);

void
  get_16color_rgb (uint8_t color_index, uint8_t * r,
  uint8_t * g, uint8_t * b)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Get RGB values for
    a 16-color ANSI index


Parameters


color_index 16-color index (0-15)


r Output red component


g Output green component


b Output blue component


Definition at line 456 of file ansi.c.


456                                                                               {
457   // Same color table as rgb_to_16color()
458   static const uint8_t ansi_colors[16][3] = {
459       {0, 0, 0},       // 0: Black
460       {128, 0, 0},     // 1: Dark Red
461       {0, 128, 0},     // 2: Dark Green
462       {128, 128, 0},   // 3: Dark Yellow (Brown)
463       {0, 0, 128},     // 4: Dark Blue
464       {128, 0, 128},   // 5: Dark Magenta
465       {0, 128, 128},   // 6: Dark Cyan
466       {192, 192, 192}, // 7: Light Gray
467       {128, 128, 128}, // 8: Dark Gray
468       {255, 0, 0},     // 9: Bright Red
469       {0, 255, 0},     // 10: Bright Green
470       {255, 255, 0},   // 11: Bright Yellow
471       {0, 0, 255},     // 12: Bright Blue
472       {255, 0, 255},   // 13: Bright Magenta
473       {0, 255, 255},   // 14: Bright Cyan
474       {255, 255, 255}  // 15: White
475   };
476 
477   if (color_index >= 16) {
478     color_index = 7; // Default to light gray
479   }
480 
481   *r = ansi_colors[color_index][0];
482   *g = ansi_colors[color_index][1];
483   *b = ansi_colors[color_index][2];
484 }

    image_print_16color_dithered_with_background(), and

uint8_t rgb_to_16color (uint8_t r, uint8_t
  g, uint8_t b)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Convert RGB to
    closest 16-color ANSI index Uses distance-based matching against standard
    ANSI colors


Parameters


r Red component


g Green component


b Blue component


Returns


16-color index (0-15)


Definition at line 413 of file ansi.c.


413                                                         {
414   // Convert RGB to the closest 16-color ANSI color
415   // This uses a simple distance-based approach
416 
417   // Define the 16 ANSI colors in RGB
418   static const uint8_t ansi_colors[16][3] = {
419       {0, 0, 0},       // 0: Black
420       {128, 0, 0},     // 1: Dark Red
421       {0, 128, 0},     // 2: Dark Green
422       {128, 128, 0},   // 3: Dark Yellow (Brown)
423       {0, 0, 128},     // 4: Dark Blue
424       {128, 0, 128},   // 5: Dark Magenta
425       {0, 128, 128},   // 6: Dark Cyan
426       {192, 192, 192}, // 7: Light Gray
427       {128, 128, 128}, // 8: Dark Gray
428       {255, 0, 0},     // 9: Bright Red
429       {0, 255, 0},     // 10: Bright Green
430       {255, 255, 0},   // 11: Bright Yellow
431       {0, 0, 255},     // 12: Bright Blue
432       {255, 0, 255},   // 13: Bright Magenta
433       {0, 255, 255},   // 14: Bright Cyan
434       {255, 255, 255}  // 15: White
435   };
436 
437   uint8_t best_match = 0;
438   int min_distance = INT_MAX;
439 
440   for (int i = 0; i < 16; i++) {
441     int dr = (int)r - (int)ansi_colors[i][0];
442     int dg = (int)g - (int)ansi_colors[i][1];
443     int db = (int)b - (int)ansi_colors[i][2];
444     int distance = dr * dr + dg * dg + db * db;
445 
446     if (distance < min_distance) {
447       min_distance = distance;
448       best_match = i;
449     }
450   }
451 
452   return best_match;
453 }






Referenced by append_color_fg_for_mode(),
    colorscheme_compile_scheme(), image_print_16color(),
    rgb_to_16color_dithered(), and
    rgb_to_16color_halfblocks_scalar().






uint8_t rgb_to_16color_dithered (int r, int g, int b, int
  x, int y, int width, int height, rgb_error_t * error_buffer)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Convert RGB to
    16-color with Floyd-Steinberg dithering


Parameters


r Red component


g Green component


b Blue component


x Current pixel X coordinate


y Current pixel Y coordinate


width Image width


height Image height


error_buffer Dithering error buffer (width*height elements)


Returns


16-color index with dithering applied


Definition at line 487 of file ansi.c.


487                                                                                                                      {
488   // Add accumulated error from previous pixels
489   if (error_buffer) {
490     // Use size_t for index calculation to prevent integer overflow on large images
491     size_t error_idx = (size_t)y * (size_t)width + (size_t)x;
492     r += error_buffer[error_idx].r;
493     g += error_buffer[error_idx].g;
494     b += error_buffer[error_idx].b;
495 
496     // Reset error for this pixel
497     error_buffer[error_idx].r = 0;
498     error_buffer[error_idx].g = 0;
499     error_buffer[error_idx].b = 0;
500   }
501 
502   // Clamp values to [0, 255]
503   uint8_t r_clamped = clamp_rgb((int)r);
504   uint8_t g_clamped = clamp_rgb((int)g);
505   uint8_t b_clamped = clamp_rgb((int)b);
506 
507   // Find the closest 16-color match
508   uint8_t closest_color = rgb_to_16color(r_clamped, g_clamped, b_clamped);
509 
510   // Calculate quantization error if dithering is enabled
511   if (error_buffer) {
512     uint8_t actual_r, actual_g, actual_b;
513     get_16color_rgb(closest_color, &actual_r, &actual_g, &actual_b);
514 
515     int error_r = r - (int)actual_r;
516     int error_g = g - (int)actual_g;
517     int error_b = b - (int)actual_b;
518 
519     // Distribute error using Floyd-Steinberg weights:
520     //     * 7/16
521     // 3/16 5/16 1/16
522 
523     // Error to right pixel (x+1, y)
524     // Use size_t for all index calculations to prevent integer overflow
525     if (x + 1 < width) {
526       size_t right_idx = (size_t)y * (size_t)width + (size_t)(x + 1);
527       error_buffer[right_idx].r += (error_r * 7) / 16;
528       error_buffer[right_idx].g += (error_g * 7) / 16;
529       error_buffer[right_idx].b += (error_b * 7) / 16;
530     }
531 
532     // Error to pixels on next row (y+1)
533     if (y + 1 < height) {
534       // Bottom-left pixel (x-1, y+1)
535       if (x - 1 >= 0) {
536         size_t bl_idx = (size_t)(y + 1) * (size_t)width + (size_t)(x - 1);
537         error_buffer[bl_idx].r += (error_r * 3) / 16;
538         error_buffer[bl_idx].g += (error_g * 3) / 16;
539         error_buffer[bl_idx].b += (error_b * 3) / 16;
540       }
541 
542       // Bottom pixel (x, y+1)
543       size_t bottom_idx = (size_t)(y + 1) * (size_t)width + (size_t)x;
544       error_buffer[bottom_idx].r += (error_r * 5) / 16;
545       error_buffer[bottom_idx].g += (error_g * 5) / 16;
546       error_buffer[bottom_idx].b += (error_b * 5) / 16;
547 
548       // Bottom-right pixel (x+1, y+1)
549       if (x + 1 < width) {
550         size_t br_idx = (size_t)(y + 1) * (size_t)width + (size_t)(x + 1);
551         error_buffer[br_idx].r += (error_r * 1) / 16;
552         error_buffer[br_idx].g += (error_g * 1) / 16;
553         error_buffer[br_idx].b += (error_b * 1) / 16;
554       }
555     }
556   }
557 
558   return closest_color;
559 }






References rgb_error_t::b, rgb_error_t::g,
    get_16color_rgb(), rgb_error_t::r, and
    rgb_to_16color().


Referenced by image_print_16color_dithered(), and
    image_print_16color_dithered_with_background().






uint8_t rgb_to_256color (uint8_t r, uint8_t
  g, uint8_t b)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Convert RGB to
    closest 256-color palette index Uses 6x6x6 color cube + grayscale
  ramp


Parameters


r Red component


g Green component


b Blue component


Returns


256-color palette index (0-255)


Definition at line 336 of file ansi.c.


336                                                          {
337   // Map to 6x6x6 color cube (216 colors) + grayscale ramp
338 
339   // Check if it's close to grayscale
340   int avg = (r + g + b) / 3;
341   int gray_diff = abs(r - avg) + abs(g - avg) + abs(b - avg);
342 
343   if (gray_diff < 30) {
344     // Use grayscale ramp (colors 232-255)
345     int gray_level = (avg * 23) / 255;
346     return (uint8_t)(232 + gray_level);
347   }
348 
349   // Use 6x6x6 color cube (colors 16-231)
350   int r6 = (r * 5) / 255;
351   int g6 = (g * 5) / 255;
352   int b6 = (b * 5) / 255;
353 
354   return (uint8_t)(16 + (r6 * 36) + (g6 * 6) + b6);
355 }






Referenced by append_color_fg_for_mode(),
    colorscheme_compile_scheme(), image_print_256color(), and
    rgb_to_256color_halfblocks_scalar().

Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.9.8
    ascii-chat
